summary_one_line: Input must carry a parseable Content-Type header value so contenttype is non-NULL and string-compared against the literal "multipart/" prefix.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The fuzz input must build a MIME part whose Content-Type is set to a concrete string (a custom 'Content-Type:' header value) so that contenttype is non-NULL when reaching line 1828; the false side of the branch then requires that contenttype be compared by strncasecompare against the 10-byte literal "multipart/". Bytes must spell out a recognizable 'Content-Type:' header token followed by a value.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving (value_profile_cmplog) seeds embed the ASCII run 'Content-Typ'/'Content-Type:' at offsets 0x24-0x37 followed by a value such as 'where'/'miltip', so contenttype is set and the strncasecompare(contenttype,"multipart/",10) call executes and resolves the false branch (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1828 shows W T=10 F=11 / W T=10 F=0 — winner takes both sides).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds carry corrupted/garbage payloads ('reee/rrrzrr', 'sslv23', random b7/ff bytes) at the offsets where the winner spells 'Content-Type:', so the parser never assembles a usable contenttype reaching the strncasecompare; DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1828 shows L T=10 F=0 (loser only ever takes the true side, never the blocked false side).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that value_profile lacks. strncasecompare(contenttype,"multipart/",10) is a string comparison whose constant operand "multipart/" is captured in the CmpLog buffer; I2SRandReplace substitutes that captured constant (and the surrounding 'Content-Type:' tokens captured at other CMPs) directly into the input at the operand offset, planting the exact bytes the parser needs in one step. value_profile has no I2SRandReplace and can only nudge bytes by coarse distance-bucket feedback, which never assembles the multi-byte header token, so it stays blocked.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side of branch 275 is the false branch of a strncasecompare against the literal "multipart/" over 10 bytes.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
Winner-resolving seeds embed a 'Content-Type:' header token followed by a value such as 'where'.
A winner seed value is 'miltip', a non-'multipart/' Content-Type value, exercising the strncasecompare.
At the blocker line the winner takes both true and false sides while the loser only ever takes the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L1828  T=10 F=0  T=0 F=10  (contenttype && !strncasecompare(contenttype, "multipart/...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds carry garbage byte runs where the winner spells the Content-Type token.
The byte-diff shows the winner concentrates the ASCII 'Content' run at offsets 0x24-0x2b while the loser scatters unrelated bytes there.
value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that value_profile lacks.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator and relies on CMP_MAP distance buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 275 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 3ada62fdafca01cd`; the 20-level ancestor chain shows the I2S-floor signal with 2 dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 18 and 19, confirming unwrapped I2SRandReplace finds in the winner's lineage (lower bound; some I2S leaks into havoc bucket).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile resolves the false side of strncasecompare(contenttype,"multipart/",10) at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog despite the multi-byte 'Content-Type:'/'multipart/' literal being required, showing CMP_MAP gradient alone suffices and I2SRandReplace is not the discriminating technique.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact parsing path that turns the input 'Content-Type:' bytes into the contenttype argument (via search_header/customct at lines 1789-1793) is inferred from source, not directly observed in the seed-to-branch dataflow.
